description_html:
The Line Follower Robot Competition, organized by the Robotics Club of IIITDM Kancheepuram, is a classic robotics challenge that tests a robot’s ability to autonomously detect and follow a predefined path with speed and precision. Participants must design and program an intelligent robot capable of navigating complex tracks without any human intervention.

Competition Format:

  • Autonomous line-following challenge
  • Black line track on white surface
  • Maximum team size: 5 members
  • Each team gets 10 minutes to complete multiple attempts
  • The shortest official time is considered for ranking

Robot Specifications (Summary):

  • Maximum dimensions: 35 cm × 35 cm (no height restriction)
  • Battery-operated robots only
  • No external communication (Bluetooth, WiFi, RF) allowed
  • No internal or external cameras permitted
  • Robot must be fully autonomous

Track Details:

  • The track consists of black lines with a width of 38 mm ± 2 mm on a white background. The course may include sharp turns, intersections, loops, and dead ends, designed to challenge the robot’s sensing and control logic.

Scoring & Evaluation:

  • The official time is calculated using a scoring formula that includes:
    • Run Time
    • Line Crossing Penalties
    • Restart Penalties
    • Adjustment Penalties
  • The robot with the lowest official time is declared the winner.

Rounds:

  • Round 1: All registered teams compete on the initial track.
  • Round 2 (Finale): Shortlisted teams compete on a more complex track.
  • Shortlisting is based on the official time recorded in Round 1.

Rules & Safety:

  • Robots must not damage the track in any way. Any form of external control, track damage, or violation of size constraints will lead to disqualification. Judges’ decisions are final.
